signals.headline: Iran closes Strait of Hormuz again after one day** | YES | HIGH confidence
signals.raw:
Iran closes Strait of Hormuz again after one day** | YES | HIGH confidence

Iran's military confirmed Saturday morning that the Strait of Hormuz has **"returned to its previous state"** of closure, reversing the country's reopening announcement from Friday.[7] This move followed President Trump's declaration that the U.S. blockade on Iranian ports would remain in effect despite Iran's good-faith g

signals.headline: Market implications:** Nearly 20% of the world's oil passes through the Strait of Hormuz daily
signals.raw:
Market implications:** Nearly 20% of the world's oil passes through the Strait of Hormuz daily.[1] When Iran announced the Friday reopening, oil prices fell 9% and the Dow surged as much as 1,100 points, but this closure reversal likely triggers renewed volatility in energy markets and equities. Data firm Kpler warned it could take "months, not weeks" to return to normal operations.[1]

signals.headline: South Korea March 2026 Inflation Confirmed at 2
signals.raw:
South Korea March 2026 Inflation Confirmed at 2.16-2.2% YoY, Up from 2.0% in Feb but Below 2.4% Forecasts | YES | HIGH confidence** [1][2][4]

South Korea's annual inflation rate for March 2026 rose to 2.16% (RateInflation) or 2.2% (Trading Economics), from 2.0% in February, driven by a 0.34% monthly CPI increase to 118.8; this missed market expectations of 2.4% and aligns with the Bank of Korea's
